title: The Ageless and Undefeated Champs of Tulsa’s Wii Bowling League
description: The U.V. Okies have dominated a competition with other senior living facilities for six seasons, but they’re not just there to win.
author: Alexandra E. Petri and Nick Oxford

title: A Humble 3-Wheel Electric Vehicle Lands Toyota in Federal Court
description: A lawsuit filed in California claims the automaker’s philanthropic arm stole technology intended to help poor farmers, but it is not clear to what end.
author: Jack Ewing

title: ‘Hard Fork’ Live, Part 3: Differing Visions of an A.I. Future
description: One final episode from our live event, featuring a debate, questions from listeners and the dramatic and unplanned collapse of a dancing robot.
author: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Rachel Cohn, Whitney Jones, Vjeran Pavic, Alyssa Moxley, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Marion Lozano, Rowan Niemisto and Diane Wong

title: Have You Bought a Tesla Recently? We Want to Hear From You.
description: Tesla sales are rebounding in Europe even though many people have negative feelings about Elon Musk, the company’s chief executive. We want to know why.
author: Jack Ewing

title: Amazon Retaliated Against Workers Who Supported Regulating Data Centers, Complaint Says
description: The employees encouraged limits on the complexes in a series of hearings in the tech giant’s hometown, Seattle.
author: Karen Weise

title: New Super PAC, the Guardrails Alliance, Aims to Rally Tech Workers to Help Limit A.I.
description: The Guardrails Alliance, which has raised $5 million, is positioning itself as a populist effort that will take on the pro-A.I. interests trying to influence this year’s elections.
author: Mike Isaac and Theodore Schleifer

title: Trump Says Apple to Buy Computer Chips from Intel
description: While neither company has publicly discussed the deal, it would be a big break for Intel, which sold a 10 percent stake to the U.S. government last year.
author: Tripp Mickle

title: Why Waymo’s Driverless Taxis Won’t Be on Your Streets Anytime Soon
description: The Silicon Valley company’s ambitions to roll out autonomous cars nationwide have hit political roadblocks in some of the country’s biggest markets.
author: David McCabe

title: Tech Workers Maxed Out Their A.I. Use. Now They’re Trying to Minimize It.
description: Artificial intelligence is expensive to use, many companies discovered. That has led to a new era of saving costs.
author: Eli Tan

title: As India Temporarily Bans Telegram, Which Other Countries Have Restricted the Messaging App?
description: Several countries have restricted the messaging app, a valuable platform for dissidents, criminals and extremists. Here’s an overview.
author: John Yoon

title: Elon Musk’s Next Move May Be a Mega-Merger of SpaceX and Tesla
description: Some shareholders might object, but there is little they could do, legal experts say.
author: Jack Ewing and Ryan Mac

title: Anthropic Employees Accuse Trump Administration of Targeting Them
description: Workers at the artificial intelligence company have been puzzled and increasingly concerned by the administration’s move to limit their latest A.I. models.
author: Sheera Frenkel, Julian E. Barnes and Dustin Volz

title: How the ‘Hard Fork’ Hosts Bring Their Tech Podcast to a Live Audience
description: Kevin Roose and Casey Newton explain why they try to include “a little chaos” when they record their New York Times tech podcast in front of a crowd in San Francisco.
author: Kami Rieck, Kevin Roose and Casey Newton

title: The Cloud Has Sound: The Unrelenting and Unseen Cost of A.I. Data Centers
description: As tech giants rush to build infrastructure, some residents who live near data centers say a constant low-frequency vibration is ruining their health and homes.
author: Adeel Hassan

title: ‘Hard Fork’ Live Part 2: Dylan Field on Standing Out in the A.I. Era
description: “I think if you have a creative voice in writing or design, you put yourself out there and you take a risk — this is a good time to do that,” said the Figma chief executive Dylan Field.
author: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Whitney Jones, Rachel Cohn, Vjeran Pavic, Alyssa Moxley, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Marion Lozano, Rowan Niemisto and Diane Wong

title: Riding High After I.P.O., SpaceX Will Buy A.I. Start-Up for $60 Billion
description: SpaceX exercised its option to acquire Cursor in an all-stock deal, bolstering Elon Musk’s ambitions in artificial intelligence.
author: Ryan Mac and Gregory Schmidt

title: D.O.J. Seeks to Halt Pollution Lawsuit Against Elon Musk’s Data Center
description: The department cited national security concerns, saying Elon Musk’s company had played a crucial role in the Iran war. It also argued it has the authority to stop environmental lawsuits brought by citizens.
author: Karen Zraick and Hiroko Tabuchi

title: A.I. Boom Ignites Asian Chip Companies
description: They make much of the gear that goes into giant data centers. Demand for their products is shifting the balance of tech power.
author: Paul Mozur and Tripp Mickle

title: UK Announces Social Media Ban for Children Under 16
description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said his government planned to bar children under 16 from social media, following similar efforts in Australia and elsewhere.
author: Megan Specia

title: What to Know About Planned Social Media Bans in Australia, the U.K. and Beyond
description: Britain said it would ban social media access for children under 16 starting in 2027, joining several other countries introducing similar measures.
author: Amelia Nierenberg
